title: ranunculus mammiformidens
intro: Ranunculus mammiformidens, a type of flowering plant, is a species of buttercup native to New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of protoanemonin, a toxic glycoside. The medicinal properties of Ranunculus mammiformidens have been associated with several health benefits, including wound healing, antibacterial activities, and potential anti-cancer properties. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used as a powder or infused in hot water to create a medicinal tea, although its toxicity and safety profile require careful consideration and expertise in its usage.
